title: equisetum hyemale subf. ramigerum
intro: Equisetum hyemale subf. ramigerum, also known as the Scouring Rush or Horsetail, is a sub-division of the Equisetum hyemale plant, a type of vascular plant that belongs to the Equisetaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and diuretic properties. The health benefits of Equisetum hyemale subf. ramigerum include its ability to treat urinary tract issues, kidney stones, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Equisetum hyemale subf. ramigerum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is often combined with other herbs in herbal remedies to enhance its medicinal properties.
